metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Codex CLI 0.130.0 ships an experimental `codex cloud` subcommand whose own help text reads: "[EXPERIMENTAL] Browse tasks from Codex Cloud and apply changes locally." It exposes `exec` (submit a new Codex Cloud task without launching the TUI), `status`, `list`, `apply`, and `diff`. `codex cloud exec` requires `--env <ENV_ID>` (a remote environment identifier) and runs the agent on a git branch (`--branch`, defaults to current) inside Codex Cloud — i.e. on OpenAI-managed remote infrastructure, not on the local machine. So the agent absolutely supports a cloud/remote background agent: `agent_supports: yes`.

For irrlicht's LOCAL daemon, none of this is observable today, for the same transport reason as the claudecode anchor cell:

- The codex adapter is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.codex/sessions, Parser: JSONL}` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex/agent.go`), watching append-only `rollout-*.jsonl` transcripts under `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/` (`adapter.go` `defaultRootDir = .codex/sessions`). A Codex Cloud task executes remotely and writes its rollout/transcript in the cloud environment — it does NOT create a local `rollout-*.jsonl` for the daemon's fswatcher to tail. The local `~/.codex/sessions/` tree only holds locally-run TUI/headless sessions.
- Process binding can't help either: the codex adapter matches with `ExactName{codex}` via `pgrep -x codex` (`agent.go` Process.Match). `codex cloud exec` submits the task and returns; while the agent runs it lives on remote infra, so there is no long-lived local `codex` process for `DiscoverPID` to bind a PID to. The read-only `list`/`status`/`diff` invocations are short-lived API queries, and `apply` merely writes a remote diff into the working tree — none of these is a watched session.
- irrlicht has no remote-feed transport. The adapter `Source` is a sealed sum of `FilesUnderRoot` / `FilesUnderCWD` / `ProcessOwnedStore` (AGENTS.md) — there is no RemoteAPI / webhook / SSE variant. Even though `codex cloud list`/`status` could in principle poll the Codex Cloud API for task state, the daemon has no adapter wiring to consume such a feed.

Net: the scenario's user-observable signals (session appears with PID = 0 / absent, state transitions mirror the remote run, session disappears when the remote run completes) cannot be produced — there is no local file and no local PID for the daemon to derive a session from. `daemon_capability: incapable; driver_capability: ready` ⇒ `applicable: n/a`.

This ratifies the existing coverage-matrix verdict (`codex: agent_supports yes / irrlicht_observes no — "Codex Cloud exists; irrlicht has no API-based watcher"`) and mirrors the canonical `claudecode × cloud-background-agent` anchor (yes/no): the agent has the feature, irrlicht has no transport for it.

## What would flip irrlicht_observes to yes

A new adapter `Source` variant (e.g. RemoteAPI) plus a Codex Cloud read surface the daemon could poll or subscribe to:
- a documented `codex cloud status`/`list` JSON feed the daemon could poll for per-task state + token spend, or
- a Codex Cloud webhook/SSE stream pushing task state transitions,
wired into a new codex-cloud adapter that emits PID-0 / no-PID sessions. None of that exists today.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: Codex Cloud is a real, shipping (experimental) feature; the gap is purely at irrlicht's adapter/transport layer, not in the agent.
codex cloud apply writes remote diffs into the local working tree, but that is a file mutation, not an observable session — it never appears under ~/.codex/sessions/ as a rollout the daemon tails.

sources.note: '[EXPERIMENTAL] Browse tasks from Codex Cloud and apply changes locally'; subcommands exec/status/list/apply/diff
Submit a new Codex Cloud task without launching the TUI; requires --env <ENV_ID>, runs on a git --branch in Codex Cloud (remote infra)
Source = FilesUnderRoot{~/.codex/sessions, JSONL}; Process.Match = ExactName{codex} via pgrep -x — local file + local PID only
defaultRootDir = .codex/sessions; ProcessName = codex — the daemon watches local rollout-*.jsonl, no remote feed
Adapter Source is a sealed sum (FilesUnderRoot / FilesUnderCWD / ProcessOwnedStore) — no remote-API/webhook variant exists
Canonical anchor: agent has cloud feature, irrlicht has no transport (yes/no)
